seoTitle: Export and Import Spaces in Confluence (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Learn how to export and import entire spaces in Confluence in 2026 using CSV migration tools, then restore page hierarchies safely on a new instance.
intro: Migrating a whole workspace risks losing page history and structure if it's done manually. This guide walks through exporting a Confluence space and importing it into a new instance in 2026, so hierarchies and attachments stay intact.

steps.heading: Open source space settings
steps.body: Open your source space, go to the bottom left sidebar, and click Space Settings.

steps.heading: Start the export
steps.body: Click the Manage Space Data tab and select Export Content.

steps.heading: Choose CSV for migration
steps.body: Choose CSV format for cloud migration, adjust target page filters, and click Export.

steps.heading: Download the archive
steps.body: Wait for the cloud package to compile, then download the completed ZIP file.

steps.heading: Start the import
steps.body: Switch to your destination Confluence site, click the settings gear icon, and select Import Spaces.

steps.heading: Upload and verify
steps.body: Drag the downloaded ZIP into the upload box, click Import, track progress, and verify the restored sidebar navigation.

faq.question: What format should I use to migrate a Confluence space?
faq.answer: CSV is the supported format for cloud-to-cloud migration and preserves attachments, tags, and page hierarchies.

faq.question: Will my page history come over during migration?
faq.answer: The migration engine is built to preserve attachments, tags, and parent-child page relationships during export and import.

faq.question: What if the export file times out?
faq.answer: Running standalone sandbox checks and handling background cloud conversions can help you bypass timeout issues on large exports.

faq.question: Will imported pages keep their original permissions?
faq.answer: You should verify space-wide role settings after import to confirm files re-lock to their proper user groups.
